task_completion.description: The ReqView application is open and the correct project is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ReqView GUI is visible with the SRS document from the 'messy_project' loaded in the main view.

task_completion.description: The 'Team' attribute column is visible in the document table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column header labeled 'Team' is visible in the requirements table view.

task_completion.description: All Hardware variations are standardized to 'Hardware'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Team' column, cells that previously contained 'HW', 'H/W', or 'Hard-ware' now display exactly 'Hardware'. This might be seen during manual editing, find/replace dialogs, or in the final table view.

task_completion.description: All Software variations are standardized to 'Software'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Team' column, cells that previously contained 'SW', 'S/W', or 'Soft' now display exactly 'Software'.

task_completion.description: Requirements assigned to 'Systems' are not changed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cells in the 'Team' column that contain 'Systems' are still present and have not been altered.

task_completion.description: The project is saved after modifications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks 'File' -> 'Save', uses the Ctrl+S shortcut, or the project title bar no longer shows an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk).

integrity.description: The agent used the ReqView GUI to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Modifications are made by interacting with the ReqView application interface (e.g., cell editing, find and replace), not by opening the SRS.json file in a text editor like VS Code or Nano.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing commands like 'sed', 'awk', 'jq', or python scripts being executed to modify the JSON file directly.
The requirement IDs, descriptions, and other attributes in the table are not accidentally deleted or modified during the standardization process.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at '/home/ga/Documents/ReqView/messy_project'. The target attribute is 'Team' (internal ID: 'team_attr'). The messy variations to look for and change to 'Hardware' are 'HW', 'H/W', and 'Hard-ware'. The messy variations to change to 'Software' are 'SW', 'S/W', and 'Soft'. The value 'Systems' must remain unchanged.
